Full job description
Job Summary

A future business ownership opportunity

Are you passionate about swimming, people, and building something you can truly call your own?

We’re looking for an experienced swimming coordinator or leisure management professional to join a well‑established children’s swimming franchise business as an Aquatics & Operations Manager. This is an exciting, hands‑on leadership role with a clear development pathway towards becoming a franchise owner and running your own business, with structured support along the journey.

In this role, you’ll provide strong operational and people leadership to help grow a sustainable, successful swim business – creating a safe, nurturing and enjoyable environment where children learn an essential life skill for life.

About the role

As Aquatics & Operations Manager, you’ll:

Lead and develop the local swimming programme, ensuring the highest standards of safety, quality and customer experience
Provide sound operational and business leadership to support growth within the territory
Manage, motivate and support a team of swimming teachers and coordinators
Use insight and problem‑solving skills to make confident, timely decisions
Help shape and deliver a business strategy aligned to agreed targets and long‑term goals
Who we’re looking for

You may already be:

A swimming coordinator or programme manager
A leisure centre manager or supervisor
A swimming teacher ready to step into a broader leadership and commercial role
Ideally, you’ll bring:

Experience working within the leisure or aquatics sector in a supervisory or managerial capacity
Direct responsibility for delivering a swimming programme or similar operation
Strong leadership, organisation and decision‑making skills
A UK‑accredited swimming teaching qualification (Swim England or STA) or willingness to work towards this
Most importantly, you’ll be ambitious, people‑focused, and excited by the opportunity to grow into running and owning a business in the future.

A genuine pathway to business ownership. This role is designed as a stepping stone towards franchise ownership. You’ll gain:

First‑hand experience of running a successful swimming franchise
Ongoing training, mentoring and development support
Clear insight into what it takes to grow, manage and ultimately own a business
Structured guidance as you progress along the franchise ownership journey
What we offer

A strong, supportive culture with ongoing training and development
Family‑friendly policies
Flexible hybrid working
Employee Assistance Programme
Pension scheme
A rewarding, purpose‑driven role to be proud of
Salary: from £35,000

How to apply

Please include a covering letter with your application explaining why you’re excited about this opportunity and how you see this role supporting your future career ambitions.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: From £35,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Company pension
Employee discount
Work Location: Hybrid remote in Staffordshire
shortCompanyReason: A well-established children’s swimming franchise business.
shortRoleReason: Hands-on leadership role with a pathway to franchise ownership.
companySummary: A confidential, well-established children’s swimming franchise business offering structured support and development.
roleSummary: Lead local swimming operations, manage staff, maintain safety and quality standards, and support business growth with a pathway toward franchise ownership.
headlineAttraction: A genuine pathway to franchise ownership with structured support.
rolePurpose: To lead and develop the local swimming programme while supporting operational performance and preparing for future business ownership.
advertSummary: An experienced leisure or aquatics professional is sought for a hybrid Aquatics & Operations Manager role in Staffordshire, with responsibility for leading a swimming programme, managing a team, and helping grow the business. The role includes training and support, and is positioned as a stepping stone to future franchise ownership.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right where my background is strongest: leading day-to-day operations, supporting people, and improving service quality in environments where standards matter. I’ve worked across leisure, healthcare and other regulated settings, and I’m comfortable taking ownership of performance, reporting and team delivery.
whyThisRole: The role fits the kind of work I do best: leading a live operation, keeping standards tight, and helping a team perform consistently. I’d value the chance to combine people leadership with commercial responsibility, especially in a setting where safety, customer experience and long-term growth all matter.
coreResponsibilities: Lead and develop the local swimming programme
Ensure safety, quality and customer experience standards
Manage, motivate and support swimming teachers and coordinators
Use insight and problem-solving to make timely decisions
Help shape and deliver business strategy
essentialRequirements: Experience in leisure or aquatics in a supervisory or managerial capacity
Direct responsibility for delivering a swimming programme or similar operation
UK-accredited swimming teaching qualification or willingness to work towards one
preferredRequirements: Swimming coordinator or programme manager background
Leisure centre manager or supervisor background
Swimming teacher ready for broader leadership role
senioritySignals: Step into a broader leadership and commercial role

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operational leader who brings structure, calm decision-making and measurable service improvement.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this role because it sits right where my background is strongest: leading day-to-day operations, supporting people, and improving service quality in environments where standards matter. I’ve worked across leisure, healthcare and other regulated settings, and I’m comfortable taking ownership of performance, reporting and team delivery.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: I’m drawn to the fact that this is a well-established children’s swimming franchise with structured support and a genuine route into ownership. The mix of training, mentoring, family-friendly policies and flexible hybrid working tells me the business is serious about building someone up properly, not just filling a vacancy.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The role fits the kind of work I do best: leading a live operation, keeping standards tight, and helping a team perform consistently. I’d value the chance to combine people leadership with commercial responsibility, especially in a setting where safety, customer experience and long-term growth all matter.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Safety, quality and compliance in a live operational environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve already created a practical tracking system in a safety-critical leisure setting, so I understand the discipline needed when standards, progression and compliance all have to be managed properly.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest leisure-sector proof point for safety and operational control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve built practical control systems where safety and progression both had to stay visible.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Operational leadership, organisation and business growth
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase, which means I’m used to keeping delivery stable while the business is growing and demands are changing.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for growth, resilience and day-to-day leadership.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led operations through growth without losing grip on delivery.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This shows I can work beyond service delivery and help improve the numbers behind the operation, which matters if the role is preparing someone for future ownership.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a proof point for commercial thinking and ownership readiness.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m comfortable with the financial side as well as the operational side.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I read this as a role that needs someone who can run a local programme well, keep children’s and parents’ experience strong, and make sensible decisions quickly. It also needs someone who can think like a future owner: organised, commercially aware, and able to build something sustainable rather than just keep it ticking over.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Lead and develop a swimming programme
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in leisure operations where compliance, progression and live delivery all had to be managed carefully, and I’ve built tools to make that easier to control.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is the closest match to running a structured programme with safety and consistency at the centre.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Manage and support teachers and coordinators
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led and supported a team through business-critical work, balancing workload, standards and training needs.
experienceMappings.relevance: That experience translates well to keeping a small team aligned, supported and clear on priorities.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Make timely decisions using insight and problem-solving
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built reporting and visibility around live operational issues so decisions can be made with better information.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs someone who can spot issues early and act without overcomplicating things.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Support growth and long-term business goals
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve helped improve performance during a scale-up phase, with stronger margin, more orders won and better resilience.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can support growth without losing operational discipline.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Commercial awareness and future ownership mindset
experienceMappings.myEvidence: In NHS primary care I helped improve profit, working capital and cash position through tighter control and reporting.
experienceMappings.relevance: That matters here because the role is clearly linked to future franchise ownership.

focusAreasToBring.title: Programme control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d focus on keeping the swimming programme well organised, safe and consistent, with clear visibility of what is working and where attention is needed.

focusAreasToBring.title: People leadership
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d make sure teachers and coordinators feel supported, clear on expectations, and able to deliver a good experience for children and parents.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d keep an eye on performance, targets and the practical levers that make the business more sustainable over time.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background lines up well with this because I’ve led operations in leisure, healthcare and other controlled environments where service quality, people management and decision-making all mattered. I’m used to taking responsibility for performance, building better reporting, and keeping teams focused on delivery.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first 6-12 months getting close to the programme, understanding the local operation properly, and making sure standards are consistent. I’d also look for practical ways to improve team rhythm, customer experience and visibility around performance, while learning the business model in a way that supports future ownership.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people are supported but standards are still clear, and that’s the tone I get from this brief. I’m comfortable being hands-on, practical and accountable.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d get close to the programme, the team, the customer journey and the current standards so I understand how the business runs day to day.
I’d focus on team routines, reporting, priorities and any immediate operational issues that affect safety, quality or customer experience.
I’d look for practical improvements I can make with the team, while aligning my work to the agreed targets and longer-term goals of the business.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring a steady hand, a strong operational mindset and the kind of commercial discipline that can support both the programme and the path to ownership.
closingProofPoints: I’ve improved service performance in NHS primary care.
I’ve led leisure operations in a safety-critical environment.
I’ve supported growth, reporting and financial control in complex settings.
contentNotes: I have not claimed a swimming teaching qualification because it is not shown in the CV or evidence bank.
I’ve used leisure-sector evidence selectively; the strongest direct aquatics proof is the Merlin compliance example.
The company is confidential, so I’ve kept company-specific content to what the advert clearly states.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
